
% Auto-generated appendix support
\appendix
\section{Data and Code Availability}
The paper figures and tables in this appendix were generated from the balanced factorial suite
\texttt{suite\_20260404T090445Z\_factorial}, which contains 600 completed runs.
Derived analysis tables are provided as CSV files:
\texttt{engine\_summary.csv},
\texttt{engine\_model\_summary.csv},
\texttt{anova\_results.csv},
\texttt{pairwise\_tukey\_by\_engine.csv}, and
\texttt{representative\_excerpts.csv}.

The code used to generate the paper assets is provided in
\texttt{build\_paper\_analysis.py}.
The original linguistic and tokenizer analysis inputs are preserved in the
\texttt{analysis/linguistics/...} and \texttt{analysis/tokenizers/...} directories.
Representative textual outputs can be recovered from the corresponding
\texttt{run.json} files in the suite outputs directory.

\section{Statistical Notes}
All reported ANOVA tables were computed from fixed-effects models of the form
\[
\text{metric} \sim \texttt{engine} + \texttt{model} + \texttt{engine}\times\texttt{model} + \texttt{prompt\_index}.
\]
This design isolates engine effects from model effects because every engine was run on every model across every prompt and replicate.
Because the run counts are balanced, the resulting effect sizes are straightforward to interpret.
For pairwise engine comparisons, Tukey's HSD was applied to the raw run-level values.
